category_title: Years 7 and 8 boys with literacy underachievement and behavioral challenges
minimal_context: The student is in a boys only class during a daily classroom programme, tasked with engaging in regular silent reading of novel-length stories.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please open your novel-length stories and begin your regular silent reading.

STUDENT: (Shows reluctance to fully engage in reading) I don't want to read. Can we do boys' activities, such as playing tackle games?

TUTOR: No, right now is the time for silent reading.

STUDENT: (Demonstrates inattention and lack of focus on tasks) I can't focus on this.

TUTOR: Please look at your book and try.

STUDENT: (Exhibits anti-social behaviour towards peers and complete reluctance to fully engage in reading) I'm not doing it.

options.A: Break the silent reading goal down into sub-targets: first week, remain seated for 10 minutes; second week, remain seated for 90 minutes.
options.B: Engage the student with novel-length stories that are sure to interest him and encourage regular silent reading.
options.C: Purposefully select novel-length stories written by and/or featuring people of color for the daily reading programme.
options.D: Explicitly incorporate and promote self-compassion within the daily reading programme content during the silent reading sessions.
